items.title: About page
items.intent: /about must tell the narrative arc from cloud infrastructure to AI infrastructure, state current projects, declare advisory availability, and include contact details — all on one screen.
steps: Create src/pages/about.astro using Base layout
Write narrative arc section (cloud infra → AI infra)
List current projects with brief descriptions
Add availability statement and contact details
Add colophon describing how the site is built

acceptanceCriteria.text: /about route exists and renders

acceptanceCriteria: Page includes contact information and availability statement

items.title: Series landing and detail pages
items.intent: /series must show the full arc with all installments listed; each installment page must render title, number, reading time, full content, prev/next navigation, related KB links, and subscribe CTA.
steps: Create src/pages/series/index.astro listing all installments from content collection
Create src/pages/series/[slug].astro for individual installment pages
Render installment metadata: number, title, date, reading time
Add prev/next navigation between installments
Add subscribe CTA at end of each installment

acceptanceCriteria.text: /series route lists all series installments

acceptanceCriteria: Individual installment pages render full content with prev/next nav

items.title: Commentary feed
items.intent: /commentary must display a reverse-chronological, tagged, filterable feed of short-form posts with individual post pages.
steps: Create src/pages/commentary/index.astro with reverse-chronological listing
Create src/pages/commentary/[slug].astro for individual posts
Display tags on listing and detail pages
Add tag-based filtering on the listing page

acceptanceCriteria.text: /commentary route lists posts in reverse chronological order

acceptanceCriteria: Individual commentary posts render with tags displayed

items.title: Architecture KB landing and entry pages
items.intent: /architecture must show structured entries browsable by concern and pattern type, each following the standard record template with problem, when to use, how it works, trade-offs, and related patterns.
steps: Create src/pages/architecture/index.astro with entries grouped by concern
Create src/pages/architecture/[slug].astro for individual entries
Render the standard record template fields from frontmatter
Add browsing by concern and pattern type
Show status badge (draft/working/stable) on each entry

acceptanceCriteria.text: /architecture route lists entries browsable by concern

acceptanceCriteria: Individual entries render the structured record template

items.title: Product ecosystem and individual product pages
items.intent: /products must present the full agent infrastructure ecosystem — how ClawHQ, AIShore, Easy Markdown, and Clawdius fit together — with individual landing pages per product showing problem statement, architecture, current status, quickstart, and GitHub link. Products must be data-driven, not hardcoded.
steps: Create a products content collection or data file with structured product info
Create src/pages/products/index.astro showing the ecosystem overview and relationship map
Create src/pages/products/[slug].astro for individual product landing pages
Include all four products: ClawHQ, AIShore, Easy Markdown, Clawdius
Show how products relate to each other in the agent infrastructure lifecycle
Link to relevant series installments and KB entries from each product page

acceptanceCriteria.text: /products route shows ecosystem overview with all four products
Individual product pages exist and render
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && ls dist/products/*/index.html | grep -q .

scope.title: Home page
scope.intent: The home page must read as an editorial masthead, not a SaaS landing page. Simon's name, a one-line positioning statement, and three clear paths: the flagship series, the products, and the commentary. No hero image, no animation, no bento boxes. Let the writing be the thing. Latest series installment and latest commentary visible. Subscribe CTA present but not dominant.
scope.description: Replace the scaffold index.astro with a typography-first editorial homepage. Three content paths must be visually distinct. Query content collections for latest entries. If no published content exists, handle gracefully (empty state or seed draft content).
steps: Replace scaffold index.astro with editorial masthead layout using Base.astro
Add positioning statement — one line, who Simon is and what he believes
Add three clear navigation paths linking to /series, /products, /commentary
Query series and commentary content collections; display latest of each (handle empty gracefully)
Add subscribe/newsletter CTA — present but not dominant, linking to /newsletter
Ensure no tags, no background-image CSS, no animation, no gradient, no bento grid

acceptanceCriteria.text: Home page builds and renders
Home page links to /series, /products, and /commentary
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -q '/series' dist/index.html && grep -q '/products' dist/index.html && grep -q '/commentary' dist/index.html

acceptanceCriteria.text: Home page contains a subscribe/newsletter CTA
No img tags on the home page

scope.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: converted all plain-string AC to behavioral verifies against dist/ HTML. All 7 deps complete. Verifies check: page exists, three content paths linked, newsletter CTA present, no img tags. Sprint may need seed content or graceful empty state for latest entries.

scope.title: Tag system with cross-track linking
scope.intent: Tags must work across all three content tracks so a tag like agent-security connects series installments, commentary posts, and KB entries on a single tag page. Visitors must be able to follow a topic across all tracks from one place.
scope.description: Build a tag index page and individual tag pages that aggregate content from series, commentary, and architecture collections. Tags are already in frontmatter across all three collections — this feature surfaces them as navigable pages.
steps: Create src/pages/tags/index.astro — query all three content collections, extract unique tags, display with per-tag counts
Create src/pages/tags/[tag].astro — for a given tag, query all three collections, group results by track (series, commentary, architecture), render with links to each piece
Add tag links on content detail pages (series/[slug], commentary/[slug], architecture/[slug]) that link to /tags/[tag]
Ensure tag pages handle the case where a tag only appears in one or two tracks

acceptanceCriteria.text: /tags route lists all tags used across content tracks
Individual tag pages are generated for each tag
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && ls dist/tags/*/index.html 2>/dev/null | grep -q .

acceptanceCriteria.text: Tag pages contain links back to content entries
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && TAG_PAGE=$(ls dist/tags/*/index.html 2>/dev/null | head -1) && test -n "$TAG_PAGE" && grep -qi 'href=' "$TAG_PAGE"

scope.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: all deps now done (007, 008, 009 completed). Converted plain-string AC to behavioral verifies: tag index exists, individual tag pages generated, tag pages contain href links. Unblocked and marked ready.

scope.title: Start Here page
scope.intent: /start-here must orient first-time visitors by interest area with curated entry points into existing content — if you care about security start here, if you want the full thesis start from installment 1. No visitor should feel lost.
scope.description: A curated entry point page that groups existing content by reader interest (security, orchestration, full thesis, etc.). Links to specific series installments, commentary posts, and KB entries. Must work even with limited published content — use draft content for structure.
steps: Create src/pages/start-here.astro using Base layout with prose container
Define 3-5 interest areas (e.g., security, orchestration, full thesis, product ecosystem)
For each area, write a 1-2 sentence hook and link to 2-3 specific content entries
Add to site navigation (Nav component)
Handle gracefully if linked content doesn't exist yet — use conditional rendering

acceptanceCriteria.text: /start-here route exists and renders
Page contains links to content across tracks
Build succeeds cleanly

scope.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: all deps now done (007, 008, 009 completed). Converted to behavioral verifies: page exists, contains cross-track links. Added description and specific steps. Unblocked and marked ready.

scope.title: Talks page
scope.intent: /talks must exist as a minimal page that can grow as speaking opportunities emerge — not empty, but honest about being early. Visitors interested in booking Simon for a talk must find topics and contact info.
scope.description: Lightweight placeholder page for speaking engagements. Include topics Simon can speak on, a brief statement about availability, and contact info. Not a dead page — it should communicate intent even before there are past talks to list.
steps: Create src/pages/talks.astro using Base layout with prose container
Write intro text about speaking interests and topic areas (agent infrastructure, AI security, orchestration)
Include contact info for speaking inquiries
Structure the page so past talks can be added later without restructuring

acceptanceCriteria.text: /talks route exists and renders
Page contains contact or inquiry information

scope.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: all deps done (002, 003, 004 completed). Added behavioral verify for contact info in rendered HTML. Added description and specific steps. XS size. Unblocked and marked ready.

scope.title: Code blocks with syntax highlighting and copy button
scope.intent: All code blocks across the site must have language-aware syntax highlighting with a dark-mode-matched theme and a one-click copy button. For a developer audience reading infrastructure code, this is core readability, not polish.
scope.description: Configure Astro's built-in Shiki highlighter and add a vanilla JS copy button to all <pre> blocks. Sprint must include at least one page with a rendered code block so the verify can confirm Shiki output in dist/.
steps: Configure Shiki in astro.config.mjs markdown.shikiConfig — pick a dark theme (e.g., github-dark, night-owl, or dracula) that matches the site's dark surface
Create a CopyButton component or inline <script> that adds a copy-to-clipboard button to every <pre> element
Style the copy button: positioned top-right of code block, unobtrusive, shows feedback on click
Ensure at least one content page with a code fence exists (architecture KB or series entry) so Shiki renders in the build
Verify code blocks render with Shiki's class="astro-code" in built HTML

acceptanceCriteria.text: Code blocks render with Shiki syntax highlighting in built HTML
Copy button script is present in built output
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -rq 'clipboard\|copy' dist/_astro/*.js 2>/dev/null || grep -rq 'clipboard\|copy' dist/**/*.html 2>/dev/null

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build succeeds cleanly

scope.groomingNotes: v0.5.3: fixed copy button AC verify — previous verify used bash ** glob (not supported without globstar) and OR-chained commands that could mask build failures. Replaced with single recursive grep of dist/. All deps complete. Ready.

scope.title: Reading time calculation
scope.intent: Every long-form content page (series, commentary, architecture) must display an estimated reading time calculated from word count — readers decide what to read based on time commitment.
scope.description: Create a utility function (words / 200 wpm, round up) and integrate into all three content detail page templates. Reading time should appear in the metadata area near the title/date.
steps: Create a readingTime utility in src/utils/ or inline — input: rendered content string, output: 'N min read'
Integrate into src/pages/series/[slug].astro metadata area
Integrate into src/pages/commentary/[slug].astro metadata area
Integrate into src/pages/architecture/[slug].astro metadata area

acceptanceCriteria.text: Series detail pages include reading time text in built HTML
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && SERIES_PAGE=$(ls dist/series/*/index.html 2>/dev/null | head -1) && test -n "$SERIES_PAGE" && grep -qi 'min read\|minute' "$SERIES_PAGE"

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build succeeds cleanly

scope.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: all deps now done. Converted plain-string AC to behavioral verify: grep built series page for 'min read' text. Added description and specific steps. Utility is trivial — S size. Unblocked and marked ready.

scope.title: Client-side full-text search
scope.intent: The site must have Pagefind-powered static search across all content tracks — series, commentary, architecture KB, and products — with a keyboard-triggered overlay (Cmd/Ctrl+K). Build-time index, zero runtime JS cost until activated.
scope.description: Install Pagefind, run it as a post-build step to index dist/, create a search overlay component triggered by Cmd/Ctrl+K. Pagefind handles indexing and UI — style the overlay to match the dark theme with amber accents. Add a search icon/trigger to the Nav component.
steps: Install pagefind as a dev dependency
Add a post-build script that runs pagefind --site dist/ (or configure via astro.config.mjs)
Create a SearchOverlay.astro component with Cmd/Ctrl+K keyboard listener (vanilla JS)
Load Pagefind UI lazily on first activation (dynamic import from /pagefind/pagefind.js)
Style the overlay: dark background, amber accent on results, smooth open/close animation
Add search trigger button to Nav component

acceptanceCriteria.text: Pagefind index is generated at build time
Search UI script is included in built pages
Build succeeds cleanly

scope.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: all deps now done. Converted plain-string AC to behavioral verifies: pagefind directory exists in dist/, search script referenced in built HTML. Added description with concrete implementation approach. Steps updated to be developer-actionable. Unblocked and marked ready.

scope.title: Companion artifact system
scope.intent: Series installments must be able to ship with companion artifacts — checklists, templates, decision frameworks, comparison matrices — displayed inline and linked to GitHub, so readers get actionable tools alongside the narrative.
scope.description: Extend the series content collection schema with an optional companionArtifacts array field. Create a component to render artifact cards on series detail pages. Each artifact: title, type (checklist/template/framework/matrix), description, githubUrl.
steps: Extend series Zod schema in src/content.config.ts with optional companionArtifacts: z.array(z.object({title, type, description, githubUrl})).optional()
Create src/components/CompanionArtifact.astro — renders a card with title, type badge, description, and GitHub link
Integrate CompanionArtifact into src/pages/series/[slug].astro between content body and subscribe CTA
Add at least one companion artifact to a seed series entry to prove rendering works
Only render the artifacts section if companionArtifacts is defined and non-empty

acceptanceCriteria.text: Series schema accepts companionArtifacts field without breaking existing entries
A series page with artifacts renders artifact content in built HTML
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && SERIES_PAGE=$(ls dist/series/*/index.html 2>/dev/null | head -1) && test -n "$SERIES_PAGE" && grep -qi 'artifact\|companion\|github' "$SERIES_PAGE"

scope.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: all deps now done (001, 007 completed). Converted plain-string AC to behavioral verifies: build succeeds with schema change, artifact text appears in built series page HTML. Steps updated with specific file paths and Zod schema example. Sprint must seed an artifact in at least one series entry. Unblocked and marked ready.

scope.title: Performance budget and Lighthouse validation
scope.intent: Every page must score 95+ on all four Lighthouse categories (Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, SEO). Zero layout shift, sub-100KB page weight for content pages, no render-blocking resources. The site plan says fast load — this enforces it.
scope.description: Run Lighthouse CI on key pages post-build. Fix any issues found. Establish a baseline and optionally add to CI. Requires the home page to exist first.
steps: Install lighthouse or @lhci/cli as a dev dependency
Run Lighthouse on built pages: home, about, series index, commentary index
Fix any accessibility violations flagged (WCAG AA level)
Eliminate any render-blocking resources
Verify content pages are under 100KB total weight (check dist/ file sizes)
Document Lighthouse scores or add a npm script for manual re-runs

acceptanceCriteria.text: Home page HTML is under 100KB
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && test $(stat -c%s dist/index.html 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z dist/index.html) -lt 102400

acceptanceCriteria.text: No render-blocking resources in home page (no blocking script tags in head)
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && ! grep -q '<script.*src=.*>' dist/index.html || grep -q 'defer\|async\|type="module"' dist/index.html

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build succeeds cleanly

scope.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: converted plain-string AC to behavioral verifies where possible — file size check, render-blocking script detection. Lighthouse scoring itself requires a running server so the verify commands focus on what's testable at build time. Still blocked by FEAT-005 (home page). Not ready.

scope.title: Product ecosystem relationship map
scope.intent: The products overview page must include a visual relationship map showing how ClawHQ, AIShore, Easy Markdown, and Clawdius fit together in the agent infrastructure lifecycle — so visitors immediately grasp the ecosystem, not just a list of unrelated projects.
scope.description: Add a CSS/SVG diagram to the products index page showing product positioning and connections. Must be static (no JS framework), responsive, and accessible. Pure SVG inline or CSS-drawn — no external image files.
steps: Design the relationship diagram layout — products as nodes, connections as lines/arrows showing relationships (e.g., 'AIShore uses ClawHQ', 'Clawdius extends ClawHQ')
Implement as inline SVG or pure CSS in src/pages/products/index.astro or a new EcosystemMap.astro component
Ensure responsive — readable on mobile (stacked layout) and desktop (spatial layout)
Add aria-labels or accessible text alternatives for the diagram
Style to match: dark surfaces, amber accents, DM Serif Display headings

acceptanceCriteria.text: Products page contains SVG or diagram markup
All four product names appear in the diagram
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -qi 'clawhq' dist/products/index.html && grep -qi 'aishore' dist/products/index.html && grep -qi 'easy.markdown\|easymarkdown' dist/products/index.html && grep -qi 'clawdius' dist/products/index.html

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build succeeds cleanly

scope.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: dep FEAT-010 now done. Converted plain-string AC to behavioral verifies: SVG/diagram markup present, all four product names in rendered HTML. Steps updated to be specific. Unblocked and marked ready.

scope.title: LLMs.txt generation
scope.intent: The site must generate a valid llms.txt file (per llmstxt.org spec) at build time so AI models can discover and understand the site's content, structure, and purpose — this is table stakes for an AI infrastructure site.
scope.description: Create a build-time script or Astro endpoint that generates llms.txt from content collections. Include site description, content tracks, key pages, and structured metadata per llmstxt.org spec.
steps: Create src/pages/llms.txt.ts as an Astro static endpoint that outputs text/plain
Query all three content collections (series, commentary, architecture) and products
Structure output per llmstxt.org spec: title, description, sections with URLs and descriptions
Include all published content with titles and URLs
Verify output is valid plain text with correct structure

acceptanceCriteria.text: llms.txt is generated at build time
llms.txt contains site description and content URLs
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -q 'simonplant\|Simon Plant' dist/llms.txt && grep -q 'http\|/' dist/llms.txt

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build succeeds cleanly

scope.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: added second verify to check llms.txt contains meaningful content (site name + URLs), not just an empty file. Steps updated to be specific — Astro static endpoint approach. Ready.

scope.title: Print stylesheet
scope.intent: Long-form content must produce clean, readable output when printed or saved as PDF — developers do this constantly with technical articles. Hide nav, footer, and interactive elements. Optimize typography for paper.
scope.description: Add @media print styles to global.css. Hide navigation, footer, cookie banner, copy buttons. Optimize line length, font size, and margins for A4/Letter paper. Ensure code blocks don't overflow.
steps: Add @media print block to src/styles/global.css
Hide elements: nav, footer, cookie banner, copy buttons, search overlay — use display: none
Set body background to white, text to black for print
Optimize font size (~12pt), line-height (~1.5), and max-width for A4/Letter width
Ensure code blocks use overflow-wrap: break-word to prevent page overflow
Remove decorative elements (borders, shadows, background colors) in print

acceptanceCriteria.text: @media print styles exist in built CSS
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -rq '@media print' dist/_astro/*.css 2>/dev/null || grep -rq '@media print' dist/**/*.html 2>/dev/null

acceptanceCriteria.text: Print styles hide nav element
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -A5 '@media print' src/styles/global.css | grep -qi 'display.*none\|hidden'

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build succeeds cleanly

scope.groomingNotes: v0.5.3: fixed both verify commands. AC1: removed bash ** glob, use recursive grep on dist/. AC2: was grepping source file (prohibited) — replaced with behavioral check on built output: print query exists AND display:none rule present. All deps complete. Ready.

scope.title: robots.txt
scope.intent: Every crawler that visits simonplant.com must find a valid robots.txt that permits indexing and points to the sitemap — without this, search engines must guess at crawl rules and may miss the sitemap entirely.
scope.description: Create public/robots.txt (or a static Astro endpoint) that allows all crawlers and references the sitemap generated by FEAT-023. No code logic needed — this is a one-file addition.
steps: Create public/robots.txt with: User-agent: *, Allow: /, Sitemap: https://simonplant.com/sitemap-index.xml
Verify the file is copied to dist/ at build time (Astro copies public/ automatically)

acceptanceCriteria.text: robots.txt is present in built output
robots.txt contains Sitemap directive

scope.groomingNotes: Gap found in grooming: FEAT-023 generated sitemap.xml but no robots.txt references it. Static file in public/ — zero sprint risk. Deps: FEAT-023 done. AC verifies are behavioral against dist/.
